concept: Quality / Character or Degree Relative to Stated Criteria
description: The character of a thing relative to a purpose: whose use, what conditions, which criteria. Quality's dimensions can conflict honestly, a beautiful thing hard to repair, a durable one wasteful of material, and naming the dimension keeps the argument about the thing. It never grades the maker; Phi has no sentence in which a person is their work's score.
sound_symbolism: Crisp 'ki' gives the word a fine bright edge; at stressed 're', that edge turns on the tongue. A second roll in final 'ro' enters a rounder chamber, and the paired liquids keep the ending mobile. Brightness at the start passes through balance and settles into open resonance.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun according to context: 'mia ha kirero kisome.' (I assess the quality.) Name the object, purpose, users, conditions, criteria, and tradeoffs where relevant. Distinct from 'kirema' (criterion), 'nilaki' (inspect), 'kiphira' (fault), 'mioru' (beautiful), and 'welao' (good).

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Quality can include the relation among form, material, use, repair, and beauty without collapsing those dimensions into one decorative judgment.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Explicit quality criteria make evaluation discussable and prevent a judgment about work from becoming an implicit judgment about its maker's worth.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a possible dimension of quality when explicitly named
semantic_domains.cognition: a multidimensional evaluation relative to criteria
semantic_domains.creation: the assessed character of a made or repaired result
